title: Lepachys columnaris
intro: Lepachys columnaris, commonly known as Columnar Lepachys or Kavacha, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepachys columnaris include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
